stem: A decision-maker must choose between two options. Option A (the premium version) yields a guaranteed return of $69K. Option B (the budget version) depends on market demand: with probability 44-62% (uncertain), it yields $148K; otherwise it yields $20K. Compute the expected value of each option and determine which is better. How does the uncertainty in market demand affect the recommendation?

ground_truth.answer: EV(A) = $69K (fixed). EV(B) ranges from $76.3K to $99.4K. Point estimate: $87.8K. Option B is preferred at most assumptions (EV_B > EV_A when P(good) > 38.3%).

solution_steps: The decision switches at P(good) = 38.3%, which falls outside the uncertainty range.
common_failure_modes: Picking a single probability and giving a definitive recommendation without sensitivity analysis
Not computing the breakeven probability where the optimal decision switches
Ignoring the range of outcomes and focusing only on expected values (risk-neutral assumption)
